title: Entering War’s Third Week, Trump Faces Stark Choices
summary: As the conflict with Iran expands and intensifies, President Trump’s options — to fight on, or to move toward declaring victory and pulling back — both carry deeply problematic consequences.

title: After Trump’s Call to Send Warships to Strait of Hormuz, Nations Respond With Caution
summary: President Trump has urged China, Britain, France, Japan and South Korea to send warships to help reopen the waterway, even though they were not involved in the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran.

title: 4 Palestinians Killed When Israeli Forces Open Fire on West Bank Family
summary: Six members of a Palestinian family went out for a ride in the car. Only two made it back home.

title: Markwayne Mullin, Trump’s Homeland Security Pick, Got Wealthier Stock Trading in Congress
summary: Markwayne Mullin’s financial dealings take on new importance as the Senate considers his nomination to lead an agency whose budget has vastly expanded.

title: Confidential Report Calls for Sweeping Changes to Track Covid Vaccine Harms
summary: A federal work group says Covid vaccine injuries deserve urgent attention, even as Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. shifts away from talking about vaccine policy.

title: Trump Administration Turns to Migrant Workers to Help Farm Labor Shortage
summary: As the president’s immigration policies squeeze an already tight supply of farm labor, the Trump administration is making it cheaper to hire foreign farmworkers.

title: State Department Cuts Price of Renouncing U.S. Citizenship to $450
summary: The fee had been increased to $2,350 in 2015, prompting criticism and legal challenges from advocacy groups and Americans living abroad.

title: Oscars 2026 Winners: Updating List
summary: The winning actors, directors, pictures and more at the 98th annual Academy Awards.

title: Oscars 2026 Red Carpet Photos: Timothée Chalamet, Kate Hudson and More
summary: See what nominees and guests for the 98th Academy Awards — including Teyana Taylor, Timothée Chalamet and Emma Stone — wore to arrive to the ceremony.

title: Sean Penn Wins Best Supporting Actor for ‘One Battle After Another’ but Skips Oscars
summary: The actor has a history with the country’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, and had been skipping awards ceremonies.

title: The Billionaire Backlash Against a Philanthropic Dream
summary: The Giving Pledge, once trendy among the world’s richest, has come upon hard times.

title: Desperation in Cuba Ignites Unusual Acts of Defiance
summary: A protest in the city of Morón in central Cuba culminated in fiery vandalism at the local Communist Party headquarters.

title: Is Latin America Ready to Abandon Cuba?
summary: Latin America’s left saw Cuba as its lodestar. Now leaders across the spectrum are hesitant to aid a nation in the Trump administration’s cross hairs.

title: In Illinois, Clashes Over AIPAC Erupt As House Campaign Closes
summary: In a Democratic primary, accusations are flying that allies of a hard-line pro-Israel group are trying to divide progressives, exploiting a broader rift in the party.

title: Guerrilla Battle Over Padlocks and Tourist Trash on the Brooklyn Bridge
summary: Love Locks left behind were one thing. But when actual garbage (like tampons and condoms) started appearing, a righteous quest to remove it was born.

title: Powerful Winter Storm Disrupts Travel and Knocks Out Power in the Upper Midwest
summary: Officials in Minnesota and Wisconsin warned drivers to stay off the roads as more blowing and drifting snow was expected on Sunday.

title: Severe Snowstorm Disrupts Travel and Power in Minnesota, Wisconsin and Upper Midwest
summary: Memphis is among the cities most at risk of tornadoes on Sunday. On Monday, the storm system will shift east.

title: In Ski Towns, a Bad Snow Year Is Worsening Wildfire Fears
summary: A record-breaking snow drought has residents worried about much more than slushy slopes.

title: ‘Nobody Owns Us’: How Plans for a Google Data Center Divided an Oklahoma Town
summary: The volunteer fire department serving a rural area near Sand Springs declined a $250,000 donation from the tech giant. Now some residents are suing to block the proposed development.

title: What Makes ‘Good TV’ on the Internet? Piers Morgan Has Thoughts.
summary: The provocative broadcaster broke with Fox to build his own company on YouTube. Now he has hired a former head of MSNBC.

title: Marseille’s Tight Mayoral Race Is a Bellwether for France’s Future
summary: Voters across France choose mayors this week. The far right is performing strongly in the country’s second city, making the contest there a test of national shifts.

title: The $500 Million Mystery Will, Signed by Ghosts
summary: A seven-page document, mailed by an elusive figure, has set off a court battle over the estate of Tony Hsieh, the former chief executive of Zappos.

title: She Was Paralyzed by a Subway Train. Today, She’s Reclaiming Her Life.
summary: The artist Emine Yilmaz was shoved into a passing subway car three years ago. Six surgeries later, she’s drawing again.

title: Timothée Chalamet Was Supposed to Be a New Kind of Leading Man
summary: He’s no longer that heart-on-his-sleeve sensitive soft boy many people fell for.

title: Why Minnesota Matters More Than Iran for America’s Future
summary: Moms donating their breast milk to strangers, dads taking someone else’s kids to school: Minnesotans showed a basic human impulse to look out for their neighbors.

title: Pakistani-Afghan War Takes Heavy Toll on Civilians
summary: Pakistani airstrikes have killed at least 75 civilians and displaced 115,000 in Afghanistan, with both sides vowing escalation and no talks in sight.

title: ‘Doctor Who’ Fans Have Fresh Chance to Time Travel With Found Episodes
summary: Two unearthed episodes, which were discovered in film canisters wrapped in plastic bags among the possessions of a dead collector in England, were restored by BBC archivists.

title: In a Wild Corner of the West, Elk Are Everywhere and Causing Conflict
summary: Where Washington, Idaho and Oregon meet, elk are straying from public to private lands, causing conflict and concern. If the Trump administration opens national forests further, it could get worse.
